reasoning: Provide a concise summary of the article's key points about the Senate hearing and related Russia investigation developments.
output: During a Senate intelligence hearing, Dan Coats and Mike Rogers said they would not discuss the president’s conversations publicly and would protect sensitive information, asserting they had not been directed to do anything illegal. Coats indicated he would testify but would refrain from disclosing information deemed sensitive in an opening hearing, with a closed session to follow. The session, billed as a prelude to James Comey’s testimony, yielded a cautious, “bunker mentality” as lawmakers pressed for more on the Russia inquiry; officials stressed there is no known evidence of collusion and Trump dismissed the Russia storyline as fake news. A Washington Post report later claimed Coats said Trump had pressed the FBI to back off the Flynn investigation, amid ongoing investigations into Russian interference and possible links to the Trump campaign, while Comey’s upcoming testimony was anticipated to offer more details.]]>
